title: Stop fighting your trading psychology and start trust your data
author: Disastrous_Hotel_574
selftext:
Hi everyone, as a developer, I spent way too much time trying to fix my mindset when the real problem was my infrastructure. I realized that most of my trading anxiety came from the fact that I was relying on manual tracking and messy spreadsheets that just couldn't keep up with Gold volatility.

I decided to build Hercul Market using Next.js and Supabase to create a cold, analytical environment for my execution. 

How this engineering approach changed my results:

Structural Objectivity: I integrated an AI analyzer to map out liquidity zones and Order Blocks. It removes the human urge to see patterns that aren't there.

Data over Emotion: This week I hit a 5.5 RR trade for 1,200 profit. I didn't close early because my dashboard was showing me the math, not the fear.

Backend Accuracy: Every trade is synced to a PostgreSQL database. My XIRR and drawdown are calculated automatically, so I always know my true performance without broken Excel formulas.

Risk Hardcoding: The system forces me to calculate the Risk/Reward before I even think about an entry. If the statistics don't support the trade, the system flags it.

I've opened a free beta for anyone who wants to move away from gut-feeling trading and toward a more data-driven stack. Link is at the top.

Are you guys building your own tools to manage risk, or are you still fighting with manual logs? 📈💻
